differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune limbic encephalitis (e.g., anti-LGI1 or anti-GABA-B)
next_steps: Send serum and CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel (neuronal surface and paraneoplastic antibodies)
Test serum for thyroid peroxidase (TPO) antibodies
Initiate empiric high-dose corticosteroids (e.g., IV methylprednisolone) if infectious causes are ruled out and clinical suspicion for autoimmune/inflammatory process is high
Perform whole-body PET-CT to evaluate for occult malignancy
Initiate anti-seizure medication (e.g., lacosamide) due to EEG findings of temporal epileptiform discharges
Consider brain biopsy if clinical decline persists and initial workup for autoimmune/neoplastic etiology remains inconclusive
